info.title: DESI Data Share — Read-only connector
info.description: Read-only access to DESI/MSI projects: list projects, summaries, compounds, ROI intensity statistics, and raw {x,y,value} matrices. Read-only: it cannot register, edit, or delete anything.

servers.description: Replace with your deployed HTTPS URL (e.g. https://desi-share-connector.onrender.com)

get.summary: List readable projects (public, plus private ones the server has a password for).
Project summary: memo, sections, compounds (name/precursor/product + mean/max), ROIs.
List a project's compounds (MRM layers) with precursor/product m/z, per section.
Raw MSI intensity statistics (mean/median/min/max/quartiles/n/sd) inside ROIs. Optional filters.
Raw {x,y,value} numbers (CSV) for one compound in one section, optionally clipped to an ROI. Capped; use downsample/max_rows. For very large data, use the local connector + CSV upload.

parameters.name: section
parameters.description: section name or id (required if the compound exists in multiple sections)

get.summary: Search the REGISTERED MRM library (compounds + transitions incl. precursor/product/CE/CV, tags, polarity, usage history) from the MRM management app. Not the per-project MSI layers. Optional filters; no filter = whole library.

Reverse lookup: given a molecule/compound name, list the projects that measured it (e.g. 'which projects measured Acetylcholine?'). Matching tolerates case/separator/polarity drift and library serial_no aliases; distinct compounds stay distinct. Returns slug/name/is_public/matched_compounds only. Private projects included unless the server is PUBLIC_ONLY.

parameters.name: compound
parameters.description: molecule/compound name to look up (e.g. Acetylcholine)

parameters.name: include_private
parameters.description: include private projects (default true; ignored when the server is PUBLIC_ONLY)

get.summary: Assemble a Waters MassLynx .exp from registered MRMs. Repeat `name` for each channel in output order. CE/CV come from the registry (not estimated); unregistered names are returned in `missing`. Returns { exp_text, channels, missing, ambiguous }.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: compound name to include as a channel; repeat for multiple channels, in output order
